entries.title: What Comes After July
entries.summary: July 4 I fly back to Taiwan. July 5 I sit the JLPT N2 exam. After that? I honestly don't know yet. Here's what I'm thinking.

entries.title: Building in Public: Why I Made My Life a Changelog
entries.summary: This site is an experiment. What happens when an engineer commits to documenting everything — the food, the runs, the code, the failures?

entries.title: The Cross-Continent Engineer
entries.summary: Working in Taiwan, the US, and Singapore taught me something that doesn't show up on any résumé.

entries.title: Eating Through Singapore's Michelin Guide
entries.summary: The guide has 45 starred restaurants and over 100 Bib Gourmands. I'm going through all of them. Here's what I've learned.

entries.title: How I Ship Fast: Habits from Silicon Valley
entries.summary: Four years in Silicon Valley rewired how I think about speed. These are the habits I kept when I left.

entries.title: Why Singapore Works (For Me)
entries.summary: SV gave me scale. Taiwan gave me roots. Singapore gave me perspective. This is what the city actually does to you.

entries.title: JLPT N2 in 90 Days: My Study Stack
entries.summary: I'm not a language natural. I failed Japanese once already. Here's the exact system I'm using to not fail again.

entries.title: Building SkyReal: Year One
entries.summary: Nobody warns you about the first year of a startup. The product pivots, the co-founder calls, the nights that never end.

entries.title: Why I Left the Bay Area
entries.summary: Three years into a career in Silicon Valley, I booked a one-way ticket to Singapore. Most people thought I was crazy.

entries.title: From Night Markets to NASDAQ
entries.summary: A Taiwanese kid who grew up eating beef noodle soup at midnight ends up shipping code in Silicon Valley. Here's how.

entries.title: Sprint Begins — 96 Days
entries.summary: 96 days from Apr 1 to Jul 5. Taiwan on Jul 4, JLPT N2 on Jul 5. This is the run-up.

entries.title: 找工作一個月Get me a job in Taiwan
